title: Slow-cooker rice pudding
subtitle: Pudding rice simmered slowly in whole milk
excerpt: Short-grain pudding rice cooked in whole milk with caster sugar and vanilla for three to four hours on low. The slow cooker produces a creamier result than the oven because the milk never boils — it stays at a gentle simmer throughout. Finish with a grating of nutmeg.
sourceNotes: Rice pudding is one of Britain's oldest recorded dishes, appearing in cookery books from the seventeenth century. The slow cooker version became popular because it produces the creamy, slightly sticky texture of the best baked versions without any risk of burning or sticking — the gentle, even heat does the work unaided.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Reheat gently with a splash of extra milk, stirring as it warms.

content.text: Use short-grain pudding rice — not basmati, not long-grain, not risotto rice. Short-grain releases starch slowly into the milk, which is what produces the thick, creamy consistency. Long-grain rice produces a watery, separate result that no amount of cooking will rescue.

Grease the slow cooker pot generously with the {{unsalted-butter}}. Add the {{short-grain-rice}}, {{whole-milk}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}. Stir to combine. Cook on low for 3 to 4 hours, stirring once or twice in the final hour, until the rice is tender and the pudding is thick and creamy. It will thicken further as it cools. Serve in bowls with {{nutmeg-whole}} freshly grated over the top.

items.cause: Slow cooker runs cool, or lid not sealing well
items.fix: Switch to high for the final 30 to 60 minutes. Rice pudding is forgiving — more time only makes it creamier

items.symptom: Pudding has a thick skin on top
items.cause: Normal milk behaviour at low heat
items.fix: Stir the skin back in — it adds to the flavour. If you want to avoid it, press a piece of baking parchment directly onto the surface before fitting the lid

items.cause: Long-grain or basmati rice used rather than short-grain pudding rice
items.fix: Short-grain rice is the only type that works here. It releases the starch that thickens the milk. Long-grain and basmati do not

content.text: Rice pudding is the most comforting British dessert and the slow cooker version is the most hands-off way to make it. Put it on before lunch and it is ready for dinner. Serve with a spoonful of strawberry jam stirred through or a blob of clotted cream for a proper pudding.
